core_principle.statement: Geometry is the structural language of the architectural engineering system.
core_principle.interpretation: Every shape must represent system logic, not visual decoration.

geometry_foundation.design_philosophy: Structure over aesthetics, system over illustration, logic over form

allowed_geometry_rules.description: All primary structural elements must follow vertical and horizontal engineering alignment.
Diagonal elements allowed only for controlled system transformation and directionality.
Shapes must be constructed from repeatable grid-based modules.
Lines are the primary geometric building blocks of all visual systems.

forbidden_geometry.reason: Breaks engineering system logic and introduces decorative interpretation
Shifts perception from system brand to product illustration brand

grid_dependency.rule: All geometric elements must align strictly to grid intersections
grid_dependency.violation_effect: Loss of system coherence and engineering perception

composition_rules.alignment: All geometry must follow strict grid alignment
composition_rules.spacing: All spacing derived from modular grid units
composition_rules.focus: Visual flow must lead toward transformation point

visual_behavior.focus_control: Eye movement must converge toward system node

user_feeling: This is a structured engineering system

strategic_goal.objective: Ensure all visual geometry behaves like an architectural engineering diagram
strategic_goal.outcome: Consistent perception of AvizSazeh as a system-based engineering brand
